label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The passage talks about the difference between economic growth and development. The question is specifically about the 1974 World Bank study mentioned in the third paragraph. The passage says: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, that wording is a bit confusing. Let me read that again. It says "little benefit to a third of their population." So if the growth was of little benefit to a third, that means that a third didn't benefit much. But the question is asking what the study found about the benefit of economic growth in these countries. Wait, perhaps I misread. Let me parse the sentence again carefully. The conclusion was that "more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, it's saying that a third of the population (i.e., 33%) received little benefit. That means that the majority (two-thirds) might have benefited more, but the question is asking what the study said about the benefit. The study was showing that growth didn't lead to development for many. The options are: A) benefit to a third of their population B) benefit to two thirds of their population C) little benefit to their people D) no benefit at all to their people The passage says "little benefit to a third of their population."
